meaning: adj.鍙戜綔鎬х殑,绐佸彂鎬х殑
examples: The caldera was enlarged by later paroxysmal eruptions ending in the one of AD 79.
We discussed the clinical characteristics and treatment respond of patients with paroxysmal kinesigenic choreoathetosis.
It has been estimated that 2.2 million people in the United States and 4.5 million in the European Union have paroxysmal or persistent AF.
The 663 patient trial compared patients with a type of atrial fibrillation called paroxysmal AF, giving half the fish oil pill and half a placebo.
